mainEntity.name: How should we handle LLMO strategy if we operate in multiple cities like LA, San Diego, Austin, Denver, and Salt Lake City?

acceptedAnswer.text:
For multi-location companies (like those spanning LA, San Diego, Austin, Denver, and Salt Lake City), the challenge is balancing a unified brand story with localized relevance. LLMO strategy must make it clear when and why each location matters.

Models need structured signals to understand your footprint and route local intent correctly.

Key factors:
- Clear, consistent location pages and entity data for each city.
- Localized use cases, industries, and case studies tied to each region.
- Thoughtful internal linking that reflects how your locations operate together.
- Region-specific prompts tested in LLMs to check routing (e.g., “Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) software in Denver vs. LA”).

Design a single, coherent entity strategy that supports multiple cities, then layer in localized content where it genuinely affects purchase decisions. A seasoned LLMO partner can help you avoid both duplication and gaps across markets.
acceptedAnswer.description: Use a unified entity and brand structure with clear, well-optimized location pages, then add genuinely local use cases and case studies for each city. Test region-specific prompts in LLMs to ensure models understand your footprint and route local intent to the right location.
